title: dumpling does not export table data with --snapshot when table has been dropped
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

When using dumpling to export a database with the --snapshot flag, if the table has been dropped after the snapshot timestamp, only the schema (DDL) is exported, and the table data is missing. However, querying the same snapshot via the MySQL client correctly returns the table data.

```
mysql> set @@tidb_snapshot='2025-09-18 11:36:03';
Query OK, 0 rows affected (0.00 sec)


mysql> select * from test1.test1;
+------+
| a    |
+------+
|    1 |
+------+
1 row in set (0.00 sec) 


tiup dumpling:v8.5.2 -h 10.1.1.3 -P 4913 -u root -B 'test1'   -o '/home/tidb/test/0909/' --snapshot "2025-09-18 11:36:03"

ls
metadata  test1-schema-create.sql 
```

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

dumpling should export both the schema and the table data for the specified snapshot, even if the table was dropped later.

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

dumpling does not export table data with --snapshot when table has been dropped

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
v8.5.2
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) VALUES (1, 'Test Name');
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE created_at < (SELECT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
key_bug_features: Export missing table data after table has been dropped
